extra_model_fields.verifier_issues_summary: Artifacts present and memo preview supports many internal claims, but the grader's semantic assertions could not be fully validated because facility_location_1.xlsx could not be programmatically parsed. The grader correctly reports this limitation and documents an assumption about global remediation costs; however, several numeric sensitivity thresholds in the memo remain unverified against the workbook and should be manually checked.

hidden_reference_leakage_check.notes: No hidden-reference leakage detected in the grader outputs or artifact previews.

metadata_json.public_response_summary: Model verifier checked grounding and score consistency.

grader_limitations: Could not programmatically parse facility_location_1.xlsx in this environment (openpyxl_unavailable). The grading relies on the expert_memo.md preview and deterministic artifact presence checks; spreadsheet formulas were not programmatically validated.

issues.message: The grader relied primarily on the expert_memo.md text preview because facility_location_1.xlsx could not be parsed in this environment (parse_status: openpyxl_unavailable, preview_error_message: 'No module named \"openpyxl\"'). As a result, any claims that require verifying workbook formulas, cell-level formulas, or computed bundles in the spreadsheet were not validated programmatically.
issues.recommended_action: Open facility_location_1.xlsx in a spreadsheet-capable environment (or enable openpyxl) and confirm that formulas for derived metrics and bundle totals are present and match the memo's calculations.
issues.title: Spreadsheet artifact not programmatically parsed

evidence.grader_limitation: Evaluation assumes the memo's interpretation of remediation costs and their application (global bundle costs) matches the intended task semantics; the author should explicitly document that assumption in the spreadsheet/memo to remove ambiguity.

issues.message: The grader notes an assumption that remediation purchase costs are applied as global bundle costs rather than per-record. The memo's conclusions depend on that interpretation. The grader flags this as a limitation but still issues a full score contingent on that assumption.
issues.recommended_action: Confirm in the spreadsheet or memo whether remediation costs (O1/O2/O3) are one-time global purchases or per-record; if ambiguous, ask the author to clarify and re-evaluate computations accordingly.
issues.title: Assumption about remediation-cost application is not explicitly proven

examples_from_memo: R1 shortfall=19, R2 shortfall=6, R3 shortfall=35
Reported break-even thresholds: R1+O2 p > 11300.51, R2+O2 p > 14916.67, R3+O2 p > 9040.40

issues.message: The memo reports specific break-even penalty thresholds (e.g., R1 + O2 becomes cost-justified when p > 11300.51). These appear internally plausible versus the memo's cited values, but they were not reproduced against the spreadsheet because the .xlsx could not be parsed. Treat these numeric thresholds as needing manual or programmatic reproduction.
issues.recommended_action: Recompute the sensitivity algebra (or open the spreadsheet) to reproduce the break-even calculations and confirm no transcription or arithmetic errors exist.
issues.title: Some numeric sensitivity thresholds reported in memo lack independent verification

extra_model_fields.verifier_issues_summary: The grader's claims are largely grounded in the available evidence: the expert memo preview contains the numeric calculations (capacity, demand, shortfalls, break-even penalties) which are arithmetically consistent with the input packet; the trace documents creation of the XLSX via a manual OOXML writer and a structural check that found formula cells. The single issue is that the XLSX could not be programmatically previewed in this environment (openpyxl unavailable), so the grader's semantic assertions about preserved formulas and sheet references rest on the trace and memo rather than an independent parse of the workbook.
